mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or plateau reversal in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.25% since mid-2006, signalling end of tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are flat/negative during FEDFUNDS declines in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST from its trailing 12-month peak signals contracting residential investment and forward-looking consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations for cyclically sensitive sectors deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1642-1649k in Dec2006, well below 2005 peaks above 2000k
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST peak-to-trough declines of >10% are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth below 100k per month and a rise in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points jointly indicate an inflecting labor market that historically precedes broad earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone carries the same signal strength.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS growth < 100k AND UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp from its 3-month prior level
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,472k (revised) and UNRATE at 4.6% in Jan2007, both still historically strong
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term's incremental Rank IC over the univariate PAYEMS-only and UNRATE-only signals is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects healthy industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following trading week as the market prices in continued expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage/revision spread (111.93 vs revised 99.83) highlights measurement uncertainty in early 2007 industrial output
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with month-over-month INDPRO growth over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) turns negative on a 3-month basis at the same time housing starts (HOUST) are down more than 10% year-over-year, the combined weakening of consumer and housing demand channels signals a broad-based demand slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; each series alone is a weaker and noisier signal.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth < 0 AND HOUST YoY change < -10%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised down from 370,418M to 355,792M and HOUST down to ~1642-1649k units in late 2006
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of joint-trigger instances, or the RSAFS-HOUST interaction adds no incremental Rank IC (delta < 0.02) versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS has remained at or above 5.0% for six or more consecutive months while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation stays above 2.5%, the market prices in a prolonged tightening plateau with limited room for near-term easing, compressing valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month; the effect requires both persistent high rates and persistent inflation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS >= 5.0% for 6+ consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% since mid-2006, CPIAUCSL revised value 203.1 in Dec2006 reflecting persistent inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL trigger periods are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the two-factor interaction shows no incremental explanatory power (Rank IC delta < 0.02) over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE level below 5% combined with no upward trend indicates a tight, healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month across most macro regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% in Jan2007, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between low/stable UNRATE readings and subsequent 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-UNRATE periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month annualized contraction in INDPRO exceeding -1% signals an emerging manufacturing downturn that historically leads broader economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets reprice recession risk before it appears in employment or GDP data.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revision gap between vintage (111.93) and revised (99.83) values underscores early-warning ambiguity in supply-side data as of Jan2007
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following INDPRO contraction triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window.
